result.markdown:
---
title: "Cell Type Annotation"
subtitle: "Step 5: Automated and manual cell type identification"
sidebar: scrnaseq
---

## Overview

Cell type annotation assigns biological identities to clusters using marker genes and reference datasets.

## Automated Annotation with SingleR

SingleR uses reference datasets to annotate cells automatically.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
library(SingleR)
library(celldex)
library(Seurat)

# Get reference datasets
hpca <- HumanPrimaryCellAtlasData()
monaco <- MonacoImmuneData()  # For immune cells

# Prepare data for SingleR
sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(seurat_obj)

# Run SingleR (cell-level)
predictions <- SingleR(test = sce,
                       ref = hpca,
                       labels = hpca$label.main)

# Add predictions to Seurat object
seurat_obj$SingleR.labels <- predictions$labels

# Visualize
DimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "SingleR.labels", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE)
```
:::


### Cluster-Level Annotation


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Aggregate by cluster for more robust annotation
predictions_cluster <- SingleR(test = sce,
                               ref = hpca,
                               labels = hpca$label.main,
                               clusters = seurat_obj$seurat_clusters)

# Map cluster labels
cluster_labels <- predictions_cluster$labels
names(cluster_labels) <- rownames(predictions_cluster)

seurat_obj$SingleR.cluster <- cluster_labels[seurat_obj$seurat_clusters]
```
:::


## Reference-Based Annotation with Azimuth

For human PBMCs, Azimuth provides high-quality annotations:


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Install Azimuth
remotes::install_github("satijalab/azimuth")
library(Azimuth)

# Run Azimuth (requires downloading reference)
seurat_obj <- RunAzimuth(seurat_obj, reference = "pbmcref")

# View predictions
DimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "predicted.celltype.l2", label = TRUE)
```
:::


## Manual Annotation with Marker Genes

### Define Marker Gene Sets


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Define canonical markers
cell_markers <- list(
  "T cells" = c("CD3D", "CD3E", "CD3G"),
  "CD4 T cells" = c("CD4", "IL7R", "CCR7"),
  "CD8 T cells" = c("CD8A", "CD8B", "GZMK"),
  "B cells" = c("MS4A1", "CD79A", "CD19"),
  "NK cells" = c("GNLY", "NKG7", "NCAM1"),
  "Monocytes" = c("CD14", "LYZ", "S100A9"),
  "Dendritic cells" = c("FCER1A", "CD1C", "CLEC10A"),
  "Macrophages" = c("CD68", "CD163", "MRC1"),
  "Plasma cells" = c("SDC1", "MZB1", "IGHG1")
)
```
:::


### Visualize Markers


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Feature plots for each cell type
lapply(names(cell_markers), function(ct) {
  FeaturePlot(seurat_obj, features = cell_markers[[ct]], ncol = 3) +
    plot_annotation(title = ct)
})

# Dot plot of all markers
all_markers <- unlist(cell_markers)
DotPlot(seurat_obj, features = unique(all_markers)) +
  RotatedAxis() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8))
```
:::


### Module Scores


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Add module scores for each cell type
for (ct in names(cell_markers)) {
  seurat_obj <- AddModuleScore(seurat_obj,
                               features = list(cell_markers[[ct]]),
                               name = paste0(gsub(" ", "_", ct), "_Score"))
}

# Visualize module scores
FeaturePlot(seurat_obj, features = "T_cells_Score1")
```
:::


## Assign Final Annotations


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="false"}
# Based on marker analysis, assign cell types
cluster_annotations <- c(
  "0" = "CD4 T cells",
  "1" = "CD8 T cells",
  "2" = "B cells",
  "3" = "NK cells",
  "4" = "Monocytes",
  "5" = "Dendritic cells",
  "6" = "CD4 T cells",
  "7" = "Plasma cells"
)

# Apply annotations
seurat_obj$cell_type <- cluster_annotations[as.character(seurat_obj$seurat_clusters)]

# Visualize final annotations
DimPlot(seurat_obj, group.by = "cell_type", label = TRUE, repel = TRUE) +
  ggtitle("Cell Type Annotations")
```
:::


## Annotation Quality Check


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Check marker expression per annotated cell type
VlnPlot(seurat_obj, 
        features = c("CD3E", "MS4A1", "CD14"),
        group.by = "cell_type",
        pt.size = 0)

# Composition analysis
library(ggplot2)

seurat_obj@meta.data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sample, fill = cell_type)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(y = "Proportion", x = "Sample") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
:::


## Export Annotated Data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Save final object
saveRDS(seurat_obj, "seurat_annotated.rds")

# Export cell metadata
write.csv(seurat_obj@meta.data, "cell_metadata.csv")

# Export UMAP coordinates
umap_coords <- Embeddings(seurat_obj, "umap")
write.csv(umap_coords, "umap_coordinates.csv")
```
:::


## Summary

The scRNA-seq pipeline is complete. You now have:

1. ✅ Processed Cell Ranger outputs
2. ✅ Quality-controlled cells
3. ✅ Normalized expression data
4. ✅ Clustered cells with UMAP visualization
5. ✅ Annotated cell types

::: {.callout-tip}
## Downstream Analyses

Consider additional analyses:
- Differential expression between conditions
- Trajectory/pseudotime analysis
- Cell-cell communication (CellChat, NicheNet)
- Multi-sample integration
:::
